chunk_summary: Defines criteria for triaging children with SAM into inpatient care for complicated cases or outpatient CMAM pathways for uncomplicated cases, emphasizing danger signs, oedema thresholds, and caregiver reliability.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for distinguishing complicated versus uncomplicated SAM cases.
Learn the danger signs and medical complications requiring inpatient care for SAM.
Recognize the role of oedema severity in determining SAM management pathways.
Identify caregiver-related barriers impacting outpatient SAM treatment feasibility.
key_facts: Complicated SAM includes medical complications like hypoglycemia, fever ≥39°C, or severe oedema (+++).
Uncomplicated SAM requires no danger signs, a passed appetite test, and reliable caregiver support.
Severe oedema (+++) is an automatic inpatient indication per Indian protocols.
WHO 2023 guidelines allow trained community teams to treat selected oedematous SAM cases outside hospitals.
Practical complications like caregiver barriers (distance, hygiene) justify inpatient initiation.
reference_quote: Severe oedema (+++) is an automatic inpatient indication in Indian protocols. The 2023 WHO guideline allows trained community teams to treat selected children with nutritional oedema outside hospital, but outpatient system reliability varies across India.
document_key_topics: Danger signs and medical complications in SAM
Role of oedema in SAM management pathways
Caregiver reliability in outpatient SAM treatment
